objects.extract: Globalization is the process of increasing interdependence and integration among the economies, markets, societies, and cultures of different countries worldwide. It can be attributed to a series of factors, including the reduction of barriers to international trade, the liberalization of capital movements, the development of transportation infrastructure, a

objects.title: Nordic Museum
objects.extract: The Nordic Museum (Swedish: Nordiska museet) is a museum located on Djurgården, an island in central Stockholm, Sweden, dedicated to the cultural history and ethnography of Sweden from the early modern period (in Swedish history, it is said to begin in 1520) to the contemporary period. The museum was founded in the late 19th century by Artur Hazelius, who al

objects.title: Leptoxis formosa
objects.extract:
The maiden rocksnail, scientific name †Leptoxis formosa, was a species of freshwater snail with a gill and an operculum, an aquatic gastropod mollusk in the family Pleuroceridae.
This species was endemic to the United States.

objects.title: Paddle steamer
objects.description: Steam-powered vessel propelled by paddle wheels
objects.extract: A paddle steamer is a steamship or steamboat powered by a steam engine driving paddle wheels to propel the craft through the water. Earlier ships that are driven by a paddle wheel under manual power are known as paddle wheelers.

objects.title: Felidae
objects.extract: Felidae ( FEE-lə-dee) is the family of mammals in the order Carnivora colloquially referred to as cats. A member of this family is also called a felid ( FEE-lid, -⁠ləd).

objects.title: Cybiko
objects.extract: The Cybiko is a line of personal digital assistants and handheld game consoles first released by Cybiko Inc. in 2000.

objects.title: Placekicker
objects.description: Player position in American and Canadian football
objects.extract: In gridiron football, the placekicker (PK), more commonly known as kicker (K), is the player responsible for attempts at scoring field goals and extra points. In most cases, the placekicker also serves as the team's kickoff specialist.

objects.title: Carcinomyces polyporinus
objects.extract: Carcinomyces polyporinus is a species of fungus in the class Tremellomycetes. It is a parasite, growing in the hymenia of various  poroid fungi, particularly species of Postia.
objects.artist: This image was created by user Gerhard Koller (Gerhard) at Mushroom Observer, a

objects.title: Turkish drones
objects.description: unmanned aerial vehicles made by Turkish companies

objects.title: Alexander the Great
objects.description: King of Macedon from 336 to 323 BC
objects.extract: Alexander III of Macedon (Ancient Greek: Ἀλέξανδρος, romanized: Aléxandros; 20/21 July 356 BC – 10/11 June 323 BC), most commonly known as Alexander the Great, was king of the ancient Greek kingdom of Macedon. He succeeded his father Philip II to the throne in 336 BC at the age of 20, and spent most of his reign conducting a lengthy military campaign through

objects.title: Fire sprinkler system
objects.extract: A fire sprinkler system is an active fire protection method, consisting of a water supply system providing adequate pressure and flowrate to a water distribution piping system, to which fire sprinklers are connected. Although initially used only in factories and large commercial buildings, systems for homes and small buildings are now in use.

objects.title: Excelsior Diamond
objects.extract: The Excelsior Diamond is a gem-quality diamond, and was the largest known diamond in the world from the time of its discovery in 1893 until 1905, when the Cullinan Diamond was found. It was found on June 30, 1893, at the Jagersfontein Mine in South Africa, 130 kilometres (81 miles) south east of Kimberley whose fame as a diamond mining center always overshad

objects.title: Mouse
objects.extract: A mouse (pl. mice) is a small rodent, characteristically known to have a pointed snout, small rounded ears, a body-length scaly tail, and a high breeding rate. The best known mouse species is the common house mouse (Mus musculus).

objects.title: List of pasta
objects.extract: There are many different varieties of pasta. They are usually sorted by size, being long (pasta lunga), short (pasta corta), stuffed (ripiena), cooked in broth (pastina), stretched (strascinati) or in dumpling-like form (gnocchi/gnocchetti).

objects.title: Biogeography
objects.extract: Biogeography is the study of the distribution of species and ecosystems in geographic space and through geological time. Organisms and biological communities often vary in a regular fashion along geographic gradients of latitude, elevation, isolation and habitat area.

objects.title: Banded Quail
objects.extract: The banded quail (Philortyx fasciatus) is a species of bird in the family Odontophoridae. It is found only in Mexico where its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland, and heavily degraded former forest.

objects.title: Narwhal
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Gengiskanhg assumed (based on copyright cla

objects.title: Zetor
objects.extract: Zetor (since January 1, 2007, officially Zetor Tractors a.s.) is a Czech agricultural machinery manufacturer. It was founded in 1946.

objects.title: Anglerfish
objects.description: Bony fish with a natural lure
objects.extract: The anglerfish are ray-finned fish in the order Lophiiformes (). Both the order's common and scientific name comes from the characteristic mode of predation, in which a modified dorsal fin ray acts as a lure for prey (akin to a human angler, and likened to a crest or "lophos").
objects.artist: Art Howard/Ross et al, NOAA-OE, HBOI

objects.title: Rococo
objects.description: Artistic style in Europe and colonies, c. 1730–1780
objects.extract: Rococo, less commonly Roccoco (  rə-KOH-koh, US also  ROH-kə-KOH; French: [ʁɔkɔko] , also [ʁokoko]), is a Western style of architecture, art, and decoration that emerged in France in the 1730s as a reaction against the Louis XIV style. It is characterized by extensive ornamentation, fluid curves, asymmetry, and a smaller scale designed to foster intimacy.

objects.title: Hynobius nebulosus
objects.extract: Hynobius nebulosus, the Mitsyama salamander, is a species of salamander in the family Hynobiidae endemic to Japan. Its natural habitats are temperate forests, rivers, swamps, freshwater springs, and irrigated land.

objects.title: Serengeti cat
objects.extract: The Serengeti is a hybrid breed of domestic cat, first developed by crossing a Bengal (domestic × wild Asian leopard cat hybrid) and an Oriental Shorthair.

objects.title: Bird-voiced treefrog
objects.extract: The bird-voiced tree frog (Dryophytes avivoca) is a species of frog in the family Hylidae, endemic to the United States. Its natural habitats are temperate forests, shrub-dominated wetlands, and swamps.

objects.title: Antlion
objects.extract: The antlions are a group of about 2,000 species of insect in the neuropteran family Myrmeleontidae. They are known for the predatory habits of their larvae, which mostly dig pits to trap passing ants or other prey.

objects.title: Fanta
objects.extract: Fanta () is an American-owned brand of fruit-flavored carbonated soft drinks created by Coca-Cola Deutschland under the leadership of the German businessman Max Keith. There are over 200 flavors worldwide.

objects.title: Shovel
objects.description: Tool for digging, lifting, and moving bulk materials
objects.extract: A shovel is a tool used for digging, lifting, and moving bulk materials, such as soil, coal, gravel, snow, sand, or ore. Most shovels are hand tools consisting of a broad blade fixed to a medium-length handle.

objects.title: Sandwich
objects.description: Food made with bread and other ingredients
objects.extract: A sandwich is a dish typically consisting variously of meat, cheese, sauces, and vegetables used as a filling between slices of bread, or placed atop a slice of bread (known as an Open sandwich); or, more generally, any dish in which bread serves as a container or wrapper for another food type. The sandwich began as a portable, convenient food in the Western

objects.title: Monel
objects.description: Solid-solution binary alloy of nickel and copper
objects.extract: Monel is a group of alloys of nickel (from 52 to 68%) and copper, with small amounts of iron, manganese, carbon, and silicon. Monel is not a cupronickel alloy because it has less than 60% copper.

objects.title: Glass art
objects.description: Art, substantially or wholly made of glass
objects.extract: Glass art refers to individual works of art that are substantially or wholly made of glass. It ranges in size from monumental works and installation pieces to wall hangings and windows, to works of art made in studios and factories, including glass jewelry and tableware.

objects.title: Haka
objects.description: Traditional Māori dance or performance art
objects.extract: Haka (; singular and plural haka, in both Māori and New Zealand English) are a variety of ceremonial dances in  Māori culture. A performance art, haka are often performed by a group, with vigorous movements and stamping of the feet with rhythmically shouted accompaniment.

objects.title: Blueberry
objects.extract: Blueberries are a widely distributed and widespread group of perennial flowering plants with blue or purple berries. They are classified in the section Cyanococcus within the genus Vaccinium.

objects.title: Wagyu
objects.description: Principal Japanese breeds of beef cattle
objects.extract: Wagyu (Japanese: 和牛, Hepburn: wagyū, lit. 'Japanese cattle') is the collective name for the four principal Japanese breeds of beef cattle. All wagyū cattle originate from early twentieth-century cross-breeding between native Japanese cattle and imported stock, mostly from Europe.

objects.title: Dance
objects.description: Art form consisting of body movement
objects.extract: Dance is an art form, consisting of sequences of body movements with aesthetic and often symbolic value, either improvised or purposefully selected. Dance can be categorized and described by its choreography, by its repertoire of movements or by its historical period or place of origin.

objects.title: Roman military personal equipment
objects.extract: Roman military personal equipment was produced in large numbers to established patterns, and used in an established manner by the military of ancient Rome. These standard patterns and uses were called the res militaris or disciplina.

objects.title: AS S1 and AS S2
objects.description: Passenger car manufactured by Stoewer between 1927 and 1930
objects.extract: AS S1 and AS S2 were two twin models of passenger car manufactured by Towarzystwo Budowy Samochodów AS in Warsaw, Poland from 1927 to 1930. The two models differed with their engines and existed in 4 versions of car body style: taxi landaulet, convertible, limousine and panel van.

objects.title: Sandpiper
objects.extract: Scolopacidae is a large family of shorebirds, or waders, which mainly includes many species known as sandpipers, but also others such as woodcocks, curlews, and snipes. Most of these species eat small invertebrates picked out of the mud or soil.

objects.title: Paul Poiret
objects.extract: Alexandre Paul Poiret (French pronunciation: [alɛksɑ̃dʁ pɔl pwaʁe]; 20 April 1879 – 30 April 1944) was a French fashion designer and master couturier during the first two decades of the 20th century. He was the founder of his namesake haute couture house.

objects.title: Camp bed
objects.extract: A camp bed is a narrow, light-weight bed, often made of sturdy cloth stretched over a folding frame. The term camp bed is common in the United Kingdom, but in North America they are often referred to as cots.

objects.title: Krater
objects.extract: A krater or crater (Ancient Greek: κρατήρ, romanized: kratḗr, lit. 'mixing vessel', IPA: [kraːtɛ̌ːr]; Latin: crater, IPA: [ˈkraːteːr]) was a large two-handled type of vase in Ancient Greek pottery and metalwork, mostly used for the mixing of wine with water.
objects.artist: Close to the Deri and Laurel Spray Groups

objects.title: Recycling
objects.description: Converting waste materials into new products
objects.extract: Recycling is the process of converting waste materials into new materials and objects. This concept often includes the recovery of energy from waste materials.

objects.title: Sprayer
objects.extract: A sprayer is a device used to spray a liquid, where sprayers are commonly used for projection of water, weed killers, crop performance materials, pest maintenance chemicals, as well as manufacturing and production line ingredients. In agriculture, a sprayer is a piece of equipment that is used to apply herbicides, pesticides, and fertilizers on agricultural

objects.title: Beer in South Korea
objects.extract: Beer, called maekju (Korean: 맥주; Hanja: 麥酒) in Korean, was first introduced to Korea in the early 20th century. Seoul's first brewery opened in 1908.

objects.title: Auto mechanic
objects.extract: An auto mechanic is a mechanic who services and repairs automobiles, sometimes specializing in one or more automobile brands or sometimes working with any brand. In fixing cars, their main role is to diagnose and repair the problem accurately.[1] Seasoned auto repair shops start with a (Digital) Inspection to determine the vehicle conditions, independent of

objects.title: Zoo
objects.extract:
A zoo (short for zoological garden; also called a zoological park, animal park, or menagerie) is a facility where animals are kept within enclosures for exhibition and often bred for conservation purposes.
The term zoological garden refers to zoology, the study of animals.

objects.title: Pork
objects.extract: Pork is the culinary name for the meat of the pig (Sus domesticus). It is the second most commonly consumed type of meat worldwide, following poultry, with evidence of pig husbandry dating back to 8000–9000 bce.

objects.title: Rosary
objects.extract: The Rosary (; Latin: rosarium, in the sense of "crown of roses" or "garland of roses"), formally known as the Psalter of Jesus and Mary (Latin: Psalterium Jesu et Mariae), also known as the Dominican Rosary (as distinct from other forms of rosary such as the Franciscan Crown, Bridgettine Rosary, Rosary of the Holy Wounds, etc.), refers to a set of prayers us

objects.title: Teresa of Ávila
objects.extract:
Teresa of Ávila (born Teresa Sánchez de Cepeda Dávila y Ahumada; 28 March 1515 – 4 or 15 October 1582), religious name Teresa of Jesus, was a Carmelite nun, prominent Spanish mystic and spiritual reformer.
Active during the Counter-Reformation, Teresa became the central figure of a movement of spiritual and monastic renewal, reforming the Carmelite orders of

objects.title: Dentalium elephantinum
objects.extract: Dentalium elephantinum, the elephant tusk, is a species of scaphopod mollusc.  Its shell is often deep green fading to white at its tip and has between eight and 17 strong ribs along its sides.

objects.title: Diffraction
objects.extract: Diffraction is the deviation of waves from straight-line propagation due to an obstacle or through an aperture, without any change in their energy. Diffraction is the same physical effect as interference, but interference is typically used for the superposition of a few waves, while the term diffraction is used when many waves are superposed.

objects.title: Eurytemora affinis
objects.extract:
Eurytemora affinis is a calanoid copepod in the family Temoridae.
E. affinis is commonly found in brackish and estuarine waters along with coastal freshwater systems.
objects.artist: Natalia Sukhikh, Zoological Institute of RAS

objects.title: Juggling
objects.extract: Juggling is a physical skill, performed by a juggler, involving the manipulation of objects for recreation, entertainment, art or sport. The most recognizable form of juggling is toss juggling.

objects.title: Hanged, drawn and quartered
objects.extract: To be hanged, drawn and quartered was a method of capital punishment used principally to execute men convicted of high treason in  medieval and early modern Britain and Ireland. Convicted traitors were fastened by the feet to a hurdle, or wooden panel, and drawn behind a horse to the place of execution, where they were then hanged (almost to the point of dea

objects.title: Honda Civic
objects.extract: The Honda Civic (Japanese: ホンダ・シビック, Hepburn: Honda Shibikku) is a series of automobiles manufactured by Honda since 1972. As of 2023, the Civic is positioned between the Honda Fit/City and Honda Accord in Honda's global passenger car line-up.

objects.title: Louis XV style
objects.extract: The Louis XV style or Louis Quinze (, French: [lwi kɛ̃z]) is a style of architecture and decorative arts which appeared during the reign of Louis XV. From 1710 until about 1730, a period known as the Régence, it was largely an extension of the Louis XIV style of his great-grandfather and predecessor, Louis XIV. From about 1730 until about 1750, it became mor

objects.title: Gloster E.1/44
objects.extract: The Gloster E.1/44 was a British single-engined jet fighter design of the Second World War, developed and produced by the British aviation firm Gloster Aircraft Company. It was amongst the first jet-propelled aircraft to be developed and was produced on an experimental basis.

objects.title: Bungee chair
objects.description: Type of office or lounge chair made with bungee cords
objects.extract: A bungee chair is a chair that has bungee cords or bands incorporated in its construction. While the chair’s legs and other components are usually made with traditional materials such as plastic or metal, the seating and back portions of the chair are made with bungee.
objects.artist: The original uploader was Hummingbird04 at English Wikipedia.

objects.title: Filter press
objects.description: Tool used to separate solids and liquids
objects.extract: An industrial filter press is a tool used in separation processes, specifically to separate solids and liquids. The machine stacks many filter elements and allows the filter to be easily opened to remove the filtered solids, and allows easy cleaning or replacement of the filter media.

objects.title: Durian
objects.extract: The durian ( ) is the edible fruit of several tree species belonging to the genus Durio. There are 30 recognised species, at least nine of which produce edible fruit.
objects.artist: Yun Huang Yong from Harbord, Australia

objects.title: Housekeeping
objects.description: Management of duties and chores involved in the running of a household
objects.extract: Housekeeping is the management and routine support activities of running and maintaining an organized physical institution occupied or used by people, like a house, ship, hospital, business or factory. The term may refer to cleaning, cooking, organization, shopping, and bill payment.
objects.artist: Ministry of Information Photo Division Photographer

objects.title: Mushroom
objects.description: Spore-bearing fruiting body of a fungus
objects.extract: A mushroom is the fleshy, spore-bearing fruiting body of a fungus, typically produced above ground on soil or another food source. A toadstool generally refers to a poisonous mushroom.

objects.title: Cichlid
objects.extract: Cichlids () are a large, diverse, and widespread family of percomorph fish in the family Cichlidae, order Cichliformes. At least 1,760 species have been scientifically described, making it one of the largest vertebrate families, with only the Cyprinidae being more speciose.

objects.title: Grape juice
objects.extract: Grape juice is obtained from crushing and blending grapes into a liquid. In the wine industry, grape juice that contains 7–23 percent of pulp, skins, stems and seeds is often referred to as must.

objects.title: Articulated hauler
objects.extract: An articulated hauler, articulated dump truck (ADT), rock truck, or sometimes a dump hauler, is a very large heavy-duty type of dump truck used to transport loads over rough terrain, and occasionally on public roads. The vehicle usually has all-wheel drive and consists of two basic units: the front section, generally called the tractor, and the rear section

objects.title: Electric vehicle
objects.description: Vehicle propelled fully or mostly by electricity
objects.extract: An electric vehicle (EV) is a vehicle propelled mostly by electric power. EVs encompass  road (cars, buses, trucks and personal transporters), rail (trains, trams and monorails), boats and submersibles, aircraft (fixed-wing and multirotors) and spacecraft.
objects.artist: Felix Kramer (CalCars). Image retouched with Photoshop and uploaded by User:Mari

objects.title: Hemp
objects.extract: Hemp, or industrial hemp, is a plant in the botanical class of Cannabis sativa cultivars grown specifically for industrial and consumable use. It can be used to make a wide range of products.

objects.title: Txalaparta
objects.extract: The txalaparta (Basque pronunciation: [tʃaˈlapaɾta] or [tʃalaˈpaɾta]) is a specialized Basque music device of wood or stone. In some regions of the Basque Country, zalaparta (with [s̻]) means "racket", while in others (in Navarre) txalaparta has been attested as meaning the trot of the horse, a sense closely related to the sound of the instrument.

objects.title: LensCrafters
objects.description: American optician and eyewear retail chain
objects.extract: LensCrafters is an international retailer of prescription eyewear and prescription sunglasses. Its stores usually host independent optometrists on-site or in an adjacent store.

objects.title: Wagon
objects.description: Four wheeled vehicle pulled by draft animals
objects.extract:
A wagon (or waggon) is a heavy four-wheeled vehicle pulled by draft animals or on occasion by humans, used for transporting goods, commodities, agricultural materials, supplies and sometimes people.
Wagons are immediately distinguished from carts (which have two wheels) and from lighter four-wheeled vehicles primarily for carrying people, such as carriages.

objects.title: Toyota Corolla
objects.extract: The Toyota Corolla (Japanese: トヨタ・カローラ, Hepburn: Toyota Karōra) is a series of compact cars (formerly subcompact) manufactured and marketed globally by the Japanese automaker Toyota Motor Corporation since 1966. Since 1997, the Corolla has remained the best-selling automobile nameplate of all time, surpassing the Volkswagen Beetle.

objects.title: Three-dimensional space
objects.description: Geometric model of the physical space
objects.extract: In geometry, a three-dimensional space is a mathematical space in which three values (termed coordinates) are required to determine the position of a point. Alternatively, it can be referred to as 3D space, 3-space or, rarely, tri-dimensional space.

objects.title: Desktop computer
objects.description: Computer designed to be used at a fixed location
objects.extract: A desktop computer, often abbreviated as desktop, is a personal computer designed for regular use at a stationary location on or near a desk (as opposed to a portable computer) due to its size and power requirements. The most common configuration has a case that houses the power supply, motherboard (a printed circuit board with a microprocessor as the centra

objects.title: Air conditioning
objects.description: Cooling of air in an enclosed space
objects.extract: Air conditioning, often abbreviated as A/C (US) or air con (UK), is the process of removing heat from an enclosed space to achieve a more comfortable interior temperature and, in some cases, controlling the humidity of internal air. Air conditioning can be achieved using a mechanical 'air conditioner' or through other methods, such as passive cooling and ven

objects.title: Paperboard
objects.extract: Paperboard is a thick paper-based material. According to ISO standards, paperboard is a paper with a grammage above 250 g/m2, but there are exceptions.

objects.title: Cricket ball
objects.extract: A cricket ball is a hard, solid ball used to play cricket. A cricket ball consists of a cork core wound with string then a leather cover stitched on, and manufacture is regulated by cricket law at first-class level.

objects.title: Slip-on shoe
objects.extract: Slip-ons are typically low, lace-less shoes. The style which is most commonly seen, known as a loafer, slippers, or penny loafers in American culture, has a moccasin construction.

objects.title: Horse
objects.extract: The horse (Equus ferus caballus) is a domesticated, one-toed, hoofed mammal. It belongs to the taxonomic family Equidae and is one of two extant subspecies of Equus ferus.

objects.title: Wooden fish
objects.extract: A wooden fish, also known as a muyu, moktak, mogeo, mokugyo, Chinese temple block, or wooden bell is a type of woodblock that originated from China that is used as a percussion instrument by monks and lay people in the Mahayana tradition of Buddhism.  They are used in Buddhist ceremonies in China, Korea, Japan, Vietnam and other Asian countries.

objects.title: Fun Dip
objects.artist: Timothy Tolle / Flickr user: tim eschaton ( https://www.flickr.com/photos/angstd

objects.title: Islamic world contributions to Medieval Europe
objects.extract: During the High Middle Ages the Islamic world was an important contributor to the global cultural scene, innovating and supplying information and ideas to Europe, via Al-Andalus, Sicily and the Crusader kingdoms in the Levant. These included Latin translations of the Greek Classics and of Arabic texts in  astronomy, mathematics, science, and medicine.

objects.title: Advanced Combat Helmet
objects.extract: The Advanced Combat Helmet (ACH) is the United States Army's current combat helmet, used since the early 2000s. It was developed by the U.S. Army Soldier Systems Center, the U.S. Army Special Operations Command, and the U.S. Army Research Laboratory to be the next generation of protective combat helmets for use by the American ground forces.

objects.title: Vacuum forming
objects.extract: Vacuum forming is a simplified version of thermoforming, where a sheet of plastic in various forms of high-impact polystyrene sheet (HIPS) for low impact products, or ABS for bathroom shower trays, and HDPE for exterior vehicle parts, plus various other types of vacuum formable materials) is heated to a forming temperature, stretched onto a single-surface mo

objects.title: dambu
objects.extract: Dambu or dambou is a dish native to the Zarma and Songhai of Southwestern Niger made from cereals and moringa. It is consumed at any time but mainly during festive occasions such as outdoorings and weddings.

objects.title: Encope micropora
objects.artist: Juan Carlos Azofeifa-Solano, Sebastián Mena, Juan José Alvarado, José Leonardo C

objects.title: Sneakers
objects.extract:
Sneakers (US) or trainers (UK), also known by a wide variety of other names, are shoes primarily designed for sports or other forms of physical exercise, but are also widely used for everyday casual wear.
They were popularized by companies such as Converse, Nike and Spalding in the mid 20th century.

objects.title: T89 Cartridge Converter
objects.description: Famicom cartridge adapter for the NES

objects.title: Plasma globe
objects.extract: A plasma globe, plasma ball, or plasma lamp is a clear glass container filled with noble gases, usually a mixture of neon, krypton, and xenon, that has a high-voltage electrode in the center of the container. When voltage is applied, a plasma is formed within the container.

objects.title: Distillation
objects.extract:
Distillation, also classical distillation, is the process of separating the component substances of a liquid mixture of two or more chemically discrete substances by selective boiling of the mixture and the condensation of the vapors in a still.
Distillation can operate over a wide range of pressures from 0.14 bar (e.g., ethylbenzene/styrene) to nearly 21 ba
objects.artist: I do not know the name of the individual who actually took the photograph.

objects.title: Sabatinca aemula
objects.extract: Sabatinca aemula is a species of moth belonging to the family Micropterigidae. It is endemic to New Zealand and is found in the north western parts of the South Island.

objects.title: Enamelled glass
objects.description: Glass which has been decorated with vitreous enamel
objects.extract: Enamelled glass or painted glass is glass which has been decorated with vitreous enamel (powdered glass, usually mixed with a binder) and then fired to fuse the glasses. It can produce brilliant and long-lasting colours, and be translucent or opaque.

objects.title: Net sack with dum palm fruits
objects.description: plant fiber bag of the Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt (Egyptian New Kingdom), 1425–1353 BCE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy (S.8427)

objects.title: Santos-Dumont No. 7 airship
objects.artist: José Rosael/Hélio Nobre/Museu Paulista da USP

objects.title: Stereoscopy
objects.description: Technique for creating or enhancing the impression of depth in an image
objects.extract: Stereoscopy, also called stereoscopics or stereo imaging, refers to making images appear three-dimensional. The most popular kind of stereoscopy is two-view stereoscopy, which creates partial depth perception in an image from a set of two two-dimensional images by using binocular disparity.

objects.title: Chandelier
objects.extract: A chandelier () is an ornamental lighting device, typically with spreading branched supports for multiple lights, designed to be hung from the ceiling. Chandeliers are often ornate, and they were originally designed to hold candles, but now incandescent light bulbs as well as fluorescent lamps and LEDs are commonly used.

objects.title: Hummingbird
objects.extract: Hummingbirds are birds native to the Americas and comprise the biological family Trochilidae. With approximately 375 species and 113 genera, they occur from Alaska to Tierra del Fuego, but most species are found in Central and South America.

objects.title: Lemon (geometry)
objects.extract: In geometry, a lemon is a geometric shape that is constructed as the surface of revolution of a circular arc of angle less than half of a full circle rotated about an axis passing through the endpoints of the lens (or arc). The surface of revolution of the complementary arc of the same circle, through the same axis, is called an apple.

objects.title: Cowbell
objects.description: Bell worn around the neck of livestock
objects.extract: A cowbell (or cow bell) is a bell worn around the neck of free-roaming livestock so herders can keep track of an animal via the sound of the bell when the animal is grazing out of view in hilly landscapes or vast plains. Although they are typically referred to as "cow bells" due to their extensive use with cattle, the bells are used on a wide variety of anim

objects.title: Red-figure pottery
objects.extract:
Red-figure pottery (Ancient Greek: ἐρυθρόμορφα, romanized: erythrómorpha) is a style of ancient Greek pottery in which the background of the pottery is painted black while the figures and details are left in the natural red or orange color of the clay.
It developed in Athens around 520 BC and remained in use until the late 3rd century AD. It replaced the pre
objects.artist: Meidias Painter (eponym vase, according to signature)

objects.title: Label
objects.description: Material affixed to a container or article with printed information
objects.extract: A label (as distinct from signage) is a piece of paper, plastic film, cloth, metal, or other material affixed to a container or product. Labels are most often affixed to packaging and containers using an adhesive, or sewing when affixed to clothing.

objects.title: Steel and tin cans
objects.description: Sealed container for storage of foods
objects.extract: A steel can, tin can, tin (especially in British English, Australian English, Canadian English and South African English), or can is a  container made of thin metal, for distribution or storage of goods. Some cans are opened by removing the top panel with a can opener or other tool; others have covers removable by hand without a tool.

objects.title: Electrician
objects.extract: An electrician is a tradesperson specializing in electrical wiring of buildings, transmission lines,  stationary machines, and related equipment. Electricians may be employed in the installation of new electrical components or the maintenance and repair of existing electrical infrastructure.
objects.artist: U.S. Navy photo by Mass Communication Specialist 2nd Class James Wagner

objects.title: Bee
objects.extract: Bees are winged insects that form a monophyletic clade Anthophila within the superfamily Apoidea of the order Hymenoptera, with over 20,000 known species in seven recognized families. Some species – including honey bees, bumblebees, and stingless bees – are social insects living in highly hierarchical colonies, while over 90% of bee species – including mason

objects.title: Japanese cuisine
objects.extract: Japanese cuisine encompasses the regional and traditional foods of Japan, which have developed through centuries of political, economic, and social changes. The traditional cuisine of Japan (Japanese: washoku) is based on rice with miso soup and other dishes with an emphasis on seasonal ingredients.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Sixgimic assumed (based on copyright claims

objects.title: Dogs in warfare
objects.extract: Dogs have a very long history in warfare, beginning in ancient times. From being trained in combat, to their use as scouts, sentries, messengers, mercy dogs, and trackers, their uses have been varied, and some continue to exist in modern military usage.

objects.title: Jannah
objects.extract: In Islam, Jannah (Arabic: جَنَّةٍ, romanized: janna, pl. جَنات jannāt, lit. 'garden') is the place of the righteous in the afterworld, their final and permanent abode.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Xenon 77 assumed (based on copyright claims

objects.title: Camellia
objects.description: Genus of flowering plants in the tea family
objects.extract: Camellia (pronounced  or ) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Theaceae. They are found in tropical and subtropical areas in eastern and southern Asia, from the Himalayas east to Japan and Indonesia.

objects.title: Ornithopter
objects.description: Aircraft which use flapping movement of the wings to generate lift
objects.extract: An ornithopter (from Ancient Greek  ὄρνις (órnis) 'bird' and  πτερόν (pterón) 'wing') is an aircraft that flies by flapping its wings. Designers sought to imitate the flapping-wing flight of birds, bats, and insects.

objects.title: Slot car
objects.extract: A slot car or slotcar is a powered miniature automobile or other vehicle that is guided by a groove or slot in the track on which it runs. A pin or blade extends from the bottom of the car into the slot.

objects.title: Square (tool)
objects.description: Handtools for marking and checking 90° and 45° angles
objects.extract: A square is a tool used for marking and referencing a 90° angle, though mitre squares are used for 45° angles. Squares see common use in woodworking, metalworking, construction and technical drawing.

objects.title: Wreath
objects.extract:
A wreath () is an assortment of flowers, leaves, fruits, twigs, or various materials that is constructed to form a ring shape.
In English-speaking countries, wreaths are used typically as household ornaments, most commonly as an Advent and Christmas decoration.

objects.title: Rocking chair
objects.extract: A rocking chair or rocker is a type of chair with two curved bands (also known as rockers) attached to the bottom of the legs, connecting the legs on each side to each other.  The rockers contact the floor at only two points, giving the occupant the ability to rock back and forth by shifting their weight or pushing lightly with their feet.

objects.title: Marcel Breuer
objects.artist: Ajay Suresh from New York, NY, USA

objects.title: Mole (animal)
objects.description: Common name for several small mammals
objects.extract: Moles are small, subterranean mammals. They have cylindrical bodies, velvety fur, very small, inconspicuous eyes and ears, reduced hindlimbs, and short, powerful forelimbs with large paws adapted for digging.

objects.title: M1 Abrams
objects.extract: The M1 Abrams () is a third-generation American main battle tank designed by Chrysler Defense (now General Dynamics Land Systems) and named for General Creighton Abrams. Conceived for modern armored ground warfare, it is one of the heaviest tanks in service at nearly 73.6 short tons (66.8 metric tons).

objects.title: Mooncake
objects.extract: A mooncake (simplified Chinese: 月饼; traditional Chinese: 月餅) is a Chinese baked item traditionally eaten during the Mid-Autumn Festival (中秋節). The festival is primarily about the harvest, while a legend connects it to moon watching, and mooncakes are regarded as a delicacy.

objects.title: Opel
objects.description: German automotive brand, subsidiary of Stellantis
objects.extract: Opel Automobile GmbH (German pronunciation: [ˈoːpl̩]), usually shortened to Opel, is a German automobile manufacturer which has been a subsidiary of Stellantis since 16 January 2021. It was owned by the American automaker General Motors from 1929 until 2017 and the PSA Group prior to its merger with Fiat Chrysler Automobiles to form Stellantis in 2021.
objects.artist: FaceMePLS from The Hague, The Netherlands

objects.title: Dress shirt
objects.extract: In American English, a dress shirt, button shirt, button-front, button-front shirt, or button-up shirt — simply shirt in British English — is a garment with a collar and a full-length opening at the front, which is fastened using buttons or shirt studs. A button-down or button-down shirt is a dress shirt with a button-down collar – a collar having the ends f

objects.title: Taco
objects.extract: A taco (US: , UK: , Spanish: [ˈtako]) is a traditional Mexican dish consisting of a small hand-sized corn- or wheat-based tortilla topped with a filling. The tortilla is then folded around the filling and eaten by hand.

objects.title: Teresa of Ávila
objects.extract:
Teresa of Ávila (born Teresa Sánchez de Cepeda Dávila y Ahumada; 28 March 1515 – 4 or 15 October 1582), religious name Teresa of Jesus, was a Carmelite nun, prominent Spanish mystic and spiritual reformer.
Active during the Counter-Reformation, Teresa became the central figure of a movement of spiritual and monastic renewal, reforming the Carmelite orders of

objects.title: Clypeaster luetkeni
objects.description: sea urchin from the family Clypeasteridae
objects.artist: FWC Fish and Wildlife Research Institute

objects.title: Agrale
objects.extract: Agrale, previously called Agrisa, is a Brazilian vehicle manufacturing company. Agrale manufactures tractors, commercial vehicles, military vehicles, buses, chassis and engines.

objects.title: Torta de gazpacho
objects.extract: Torta de gazpacho is a type of torta, or flat bread, used to prepare a dish called gazpacho or gazpacho manchego in La Mancha and Southeast Spain, including Murcia and parts of the autonomous community of Valencia. The word gazpacho comes from the Latin adjective caccabaceus, derived from caccabus ('cauldron'), attested in several works by Tertulian, Zeno of

objects.title: Polar bear
objects.description: Species of bear native to the Arctic
objects.extract: The polar bear (Ursus maritimus) is a large bear native to the Arctic and nearby areas. It is closely related to the brown bear, and the two species can interbreed.
objects.artist: Klaus Rassinger und Gerhard Cammerer, Museum Wiesbaden

objects.title: Bárány chair
objects.description: Device used for aerospace physiology training
objects.extract: The Barany chair or Bárány chair is a device used for aerospace physiology training, particularly for student pilots.
objects.artist: U.S. Air Force photo/ Bobby Jones

objects.title: Caterpillar D9
objects.extract: The Caterpillar D9 is a large track-type tractor designed and manufactured by Caterpillar Inc. It is usually sold as a bulldozer equipped with a detachable large blade and a rear ripper attachment.

objects.title: Basset horn
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (M112)

objects.title: Quilt
objects.description: Bedcover made of multiple layers of fabric
objects.extract: A quilt is a multi-layered textile, traditionally composed of two or more layers of fabric or fiber. Commonly three layers are used with a filler material.

objects.title: Ice cream
objects.extract: Ice cream is a frozen dessert typically made from milk or cream that has been flavoured with a sweetener, either sugar or an alternative, and a spice, such as cocoa or vanilla, or with fruit, such as strawberries or peaches. Food colouring is sometimes added in addition to stabilizers.

objects.title: Swordfish
objects.description: Fish which has a long, pointed bill
objects.extract: The swordfish (Xiphias gladius), also known as the broadbill in some countries, are large, highly migratory predatory fish characterized by a long, flat, sword-like, pointed bill. They are the sole member of the family Xiphiidae.

objects.title: Orrery
objects.description: Mechanical model of the Solar System
objects.extract: An orrery ( ) is a mechanical model of the Solar System that illustrates or predicts the relative positions and motions of the planets and moons, usually according to the heliocentric model. It may also represent the relative sizes of these bodies; however, since accurate scaling is often not practical due to the actual large ratio differences, it may use a

objects.title: Beetle
objects.extract: Beetles are insects that form the order Coleoptera (), in the superorder Holometabola. Their front pair of wings are hardened into wing-cases, elytra, distinguishing them from most other insects.

objects.title: New Balance
objects.extract: New Balance Athletics, Inc. (NB), doing business as New Balance, is an American multinational footwear and apparel corporation.

objects.title: Honda Civic
objects.extract: The Honda Civic (Japanese: ホンダ・シビック, Hepburn: Honda Shibikku) is a series of automobiles manufactured by Honda since 1972. As of 2023, the Civic is positioned between the Honda Fit/City and Honda Accord in Honda's global passenger car line-up.

objects.title: La Palma (DO)
objects.extract: La Palma is a Spanish Denominación de Origen Protegida (DOP) for wines that covers the entire island of La Palma, in the Canary Islands, Spain. It acquired its DO in 1994.

objects.title: Water heating
objects.description: Thermodynamic process that uses energy sources to heat water
objects.extract: Water heating is a heat transfer process that uses an energy source to heat water above its initial temperature. Typical domestic uses of hot water include cooking, cleaning, bathing, and space heating.

objects.title: Coconut
objects.description: Species of plant in the palm family
objects.extract: The coconut (Cocos nucifera) is a member of the palm family (Arecaceae) and the only living species of the genus Cocos. The term "coconut" (or the archaic "cocoanut") can denote the whole coconut palm tree or the large hard fruit.

objects.title: Australian Defence Force
objects.extract: The Australian Defence Force (ADF) is the military organisation responsible for the defence of Australia and its national interests. It consists of three services: the Royal Australian Navy (RAN), the Australian Army and the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF).

objects.title: Entremet
objects.extract: An entremet or entremets (; French: [ɑ̃tʁəmɛ]; from Old French, literally meaning "between servings") in Medieval French cuisine referred to dishes served between the courses of the meal, often illusion foods and edible scenic displays.  The term additionally referred to performances and entertainments presented between the courses.
objects.artist: Barthélémy l'Anglais, Livre des propriétés des choses, 15th century.

objects.title: Pipe, bird
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (F721)

objects.title: Bicycle pedal
objects.description: Bicycle part which the rider pushes with their foot to turn the wheels
objects.extract: The pedal is the part of a bicycle that the rider pushes with their foot to propel the vehicle. It provides the connection between the cyclist's foot or shoe and the crank allowing the leg to turn the bottom bracket spindle and propel the bicycle's wheels.

objects.title: Senegal Thick-knee
objects.extract: The Senegal thick-knee (Burhinus senegalensis) is a stone-curlew, a group of waders in the family Burhinidae. Their vernacular scientific name refers to the prominent joints in the long yellow or greenish legs.

objects.title: Hokusai
objects.extract: Katsushika Hokusai (葛飾 北斎; c. 31 October 1760 – 10 May 1849) was a Japanese ukiyo-e artist of the Edo period, active as a painter and printmaker. His woodblock print series Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji includes the iconic print The Great Wave off Kanagawa.

objects.title: Cavalier King Charles Spaniel
objects.description: British breed of toy dog spaniel
objects.extract: The Cavalier King Charles Spaniel (CKCS) is a British breed of toy dog of spaniel type. Four colours are recognised: Blenheim (chestnut and white), tricolour (black/white/tan), black and tan, and ruby; the coat is smooth and silky.

objects.title: Bullfighting
objects.description: Physical contest involving a matador and a bull
objects.extract: Bullfighting is a physical contest that involves a bullfighter attempting to subdue, immobilize, or kill a bull, usually according to a set of rules, guidelines, or cultural expectations. Bullfighting can also loosely refer to other spectacles in which two bulls are pitted against each other, that is, bull wrestling.

objects.title: Electronic musical instrument
objects.description: Instrument that uses electronic circuits to make sound
objects.extract: An electronic musical instrument or electrophone is a musical instrument that produces sound using electronic circuitry. Such an instrument sounds by outputting an electrical, electronic or digital audio signal that ultimately is plugged into a power amplifier which drives a loudspeaker, creating the sound heard by the performer and listener.

objects.title: Airship
objects.extract: An airship, dirigible balloon or dirigible is a type of aerostat (lighter-than-air) aircraft that can navigate through the air flying under its own power. Aerostats use buoyancy from a lifting gas that is less dense than the surrounding air to achieve the lift needed to stay airborne.

objects.title: Felt
objects.extract: Felt is a textile that is produced by matting, condensing, and pressing fibers together. Felt can be made of natural fibers such as wool or animal fur, or from synthetic fibers such as petroleum-based acrylic or acrylonitrile or wood pulp–based rayon.
objects.artist: The original uploader was Lily15 at Hungarian Wikipedia.

objects.title: Vehicle registration plate
objects.extract: A vehicle registration plate, also known as a number plate (British, Indian, Pakistani, Australian, and New Zealand English), license plate (American English) or licence plate (Canadian English), is a metal or plastic plate attached to a motor vehicle or trailer for official identification purposes. All countries require registration plates for commercial ro

objects.title: Xiamen
objects.extract: Xiamen, historically romanized as Amoy, is a sub-provincial city in southeastern Fujian, People's Republic of China, beside the Taiwan Strait. It is divided into six districts: Huli, Siming, Jimei, Tong'an, Haicang, and Xiang'an.
objects.artist: Drawn by Thomas Allom, from a sketch by Lieut. White, Royal Marines. Engraved by

objects.title: List of citrus fruits
objects.artist: Flickr user: Xenocryst @ Antares Scorpii ( https://www.flickr.com/photos/antares

objects.title: Catamaran
objects.description: Watercraft with two parallel hulls of equal size
objects.extract: A catamaran () (informally, a "cat") is a watercraft with two parallel hulls of equal size. The wide distance between a catamaran's hulls imparts stability through resistance to rolling and overturning; no ballast is required.

objects.title: Tiffany lamp
objects.description: Type of lamp with colorful glass shade
objects.extract: A Tiffany lamp is a type of lamp with a glass shade designed by Louis Comfort Tiffany and collaborators, including Clara Driscoll, made in his design studio in Corona, Queens, New York City. The Tiffany glass in the lampshades is put together with the copper-foil technique instead of lead came, the classic technique for stained glass windows.

objects.title: Farmall 340
objects.extract: The Farmall 340 is a medium-sized row-crop tractor, first produced as part of the Farmall line by International Harvester in 1957. The 340 was a completely new design, unrelated to its predecessor the Farmall 350.

objects.title: Sumer
objects.description: Ancient Mesopotamian civilization from 3300 to 1900 BC
objects.extract: Sumer ( SOO-mər) is the earliest known civilization, located in the historical region of southern Mesopotamia (now south-central Iraq), emerging during the Chalcolithic and early Bronze Ages between the 5th and 4th millennium BC. Like nearby Elam, it is one of the cradles of civilization, along with Egypt, the Indus Valley, the Erligang culture of the Yellow

objects.title: Ludo
objects.extract: Ludo (; from Latin  ludo '[I] play') is a strategy-based board game for two to four players, in which the players race their four tokens from start to finish according to the rolls of a single die. Ludo shares characteristics with other cross-and-circle games from around the world; these types of games include the pre-Columbian Mesoamerican game Patolli, and

objects.title: Bell
objects.extract: A bell /ˈbɛl/ () is a directly struck idiophone percussion instrument. Most bells have the shape of a hollow cup that—when struck—vibrates in a single strong strike tone, with its sides forming an efficient resonator.

objects.title: Basketball
objects.extract: Basketball is a team sport in which two teams of five players each (excluding substitutes), opposing one another on a rectangular court, compete with the primary objective of shooting a basketball through a hoop (a basket mounted to a backboard) at each end of the court. Teams alternate between offense, when they attempt to score, and defense, when they try

objects.title: Lemon
objects.extract: The lemon (Citrus × limon) is a species of small evergreen tree in the Citrus genus of the flowering plant family Rutaceae. A true lemon is a hybrid of the citron and the bitter orange.

objects.title: Dan Rice
objects.extract: Dan Rice (January 23, 1823 – February 22, 1900) was an American entertainer of many talents, most famously as a clown, who was active before the American Civil War. At the height of his career, Rice was a household name.

objects.title: Hookah
objects.extract: A hookah (also see other names), shisha, or waterpipe is a single- or multi-stemmed instrument for heating or vaporizing and then smoking either tobacco, flavored tobacco (often muʽassel), or sometimes cannabis, hashish and opium. The smoke is passed through a water basin—often glass-based—before inhalation.

objects.title: Room 306, display case 10
objects.description: display case 10 in room 306 of the Near Eastern Antiquities in the Louvre

objects.title: Odysseus
objects.extract: In Greek and Roman mythology, Odysseus ( ; Ancient Greek: Ὀδυσσεύς, Ὀδυσεύς, romanized: Odysseús, Odyseús, IPA: [o.dy(s).sěu̯s]), also known by the Latin variant Ulysses (, UK also ; Latin: Ulysses, Ulixes), is a legendary Greek king of Ithaca and the hero of Homer's epic poem, the Odyssey. Odysseus also plays a key role in Homer's Iliad and other works in t

objects.title: Pressure measurement
objects.extract: Pressure measurement is the measurement of an applied force per unit area by a fluid (liquid or gas) on a surface. Pressure is typically expressed in units of pascals in the International System of Units (SI).
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Ytrottier assumed (based on copyright claim

objects.title: Jew's harp
objects.extract: The Jew's harp, also known as jaw harp, juice harp, or mouth harp, is a lamellophone instrument, consisting of a flexible metal or bamboo tongue or reed attached to a frame. Despite the colloquial name, the Jew's harp most likely originated in China, with the earliest known Jew's harps dating back 4,000 years ago from Shaanxi.

objects.title: Chinook salmon
objects.extract: The Chinook salmon  (Oncorhynchus tshawytscha) is the largest and most valuable species of Pacific salmon. Its common name is derived from the Chinookan peoples.

objects.title: Fleet management
objects.extract:
Fleet management is the management of:

Commercial motor vehicles such as cars, vans, trucks, specialist vehicles (such as mobile construction machinery), forklifts, and trailers
Private vehicles used for work purposes (the 'grey fleet')
Aviation machinery such as aircraft (planes and helicopters)
Ships
Rail cars
Non-powered assets such as generators, tanks,

objects.title: Casaquinho de Bebê de Boneca
objects.description: Item of the collection of toys of the Museu Paulista - 3 of 5

objects.title: Cookware and bakeware
objects.extract: Cookware and bakeware is food preparation equipment, such as cooking pots, pans, baking sheets etc. used in kitchens.
objects.artist: The original uploader was Wilfred Pau at Chinese Wikipedia.

objects.title: Magic wand
objects.description: magic wand at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 31.3.31)

objects.title: Avia 40-P
objects.extract: The Avia 40-P was intended as an economical glider suitable for clubs and individuals  but with a competitive performance capable of record setting.

objects.title: Peters's Epauletted Fruit Bat
objects.extract: The Peters's epauletted fruit bat (Epomophorus crypturus) is a species of megabat in the family Pteropodidae. It is found in Angola, Botswana, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Eswatini, Malawi, Mozambique, Namibia, Tanzania, Zambia, and Zimbabwe.

objects.title: Laser rangefinder
objects.description: Range finding device that uses a laser beam to determine the distance to an object
objects.extract: A laser rangefinder, also known as a laser telemeter or laser distance meter, is a rangefinder that uses a laser beam to determine the distance to an object. The most common form of laser rangefinder operates on the time of flight principle by sending a laser pulse in a narrow beam towards the object and measuring the time taken by the pulse to be reflected

objects.title: Strawberry cake
objects.extract: Strawberry cake is a cake that uses strawberry as a primary ingredient. Strawberries may be used in the cake batter, atop the cake, and in the frosting.

objects.title: Gargoyle
objects.extract: In architecture, and specifically Gothic architecture, a gargoyle () is a carved or formed grotesque with a spout designed to convey water from a roof and away from the side of a building, thereby preventing it from running down masonry walls and eroding the mortar between. Architects often used multiple gargoyles on a building to divide the flow of rainwate

objects.title: Lawn mower
objects.extract: A lawn mower (also known as a grass cutter or simply mower, also often spelled lawnmower) is a device utilizing one or more revolving blades (or a reel) to cut a grass surface to an even height. The height of the cut grass may be fixed by the mower's design but generally is adjustable by the operator, typically by a single master lever or by a mechanism on e

objects.title: Peruvian Seaside Cinclodes
objects.extract: The Peruvian seaside cinclodes or surf cinclodes (Cinclodes taczanowskii) is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is endemic to Peru.

objects.title: Hearing aid
objects.extract: A hearing aid is a device designed to improve hearing by making sound audible to a person with hearing loss. Hearing aids are classified as medical devices in most countries, and regulated by the respective regulations.

objects.title: Room 636, display case 1
objects.description: display case 1 in Room 636 of the Louvre

objects.title: Necktie
objects.description: Clothing item worn around the neck
objects.extract: A necktie (American English) – also called a long tie or, more usually, simply a tie (Commonwealth English) – is a cloth article of formal neckwear or office attire worn for decorative or symbolic purposes, knotted at the throat, resting under a folded shirt collar, and usually draped down the chest. On rare occasions, neckties are worn above a winged shirt

objects.title: Centrostephanus rodgersii
objects.extract: Centrostephanus rodgersii, commonly known as the long-spined sea urchin, is a species of sea urchin of the family Diadematidae.

objects.title: Drum kit
objects.description: Musical instrument consisting of a collection of percussion instruments
objects.extract: A drum kit, acoustic drum kit or drum set (also known as a trap set or simply drums in popular music and jazz contexts) is a musical instrument consisting of a collection of percussion instruments arranged to be played by a single musician. It typically includes drums, cymbals, and sometimes other auxiliary percussion instruments, such as a tambourine or a c
objects.artist:
V-drums.jpg: Ryan Oelke from Boulder, CO, USA
derivative work: Saibo (Δ)

objects.title: Lists of foods
objects.description: Categorically organized list of food items
objects.extract: This is a  categorically organized list of foods. Food is any substance consumed to provide nutritional support for the body.

objects.title: Alexander the Great
objects.description: King of Macedon from 336 to 323 BC
objects.extract: Alexander III of Macedon (Ancient Greek: Ἀλέξανδρος, romanized: Aléxandros; 20/21 July 356 BC – 10/11 June 323 BC), most commonly known as Alexander the Great, was king of the ancient Greek kingdom of Macedon. He succeeded his father Philip II to the throne in 336 BC at the age of 20, and spent most of his reign conducting a lengthy military campaign through

objects.title: Cabernet Sauvignon
objects.extract: Cabernet Sauvignon (French: [kabɛʁnɛ soviɲɔ̃]) is one of the world's most widely recognized red wine grape varieties. It is grown in nearly every major wine producing country among a diverse spectrum of climates from Australia and  British Columbia, Canada to Lebanon's Beqaa Valley.

objects.title: Container crane
objects.extract:
A container crane, also known as a container-handling gantry crane or ship-to-shore (STS) crane, is a type of large dockside gantry crane found at container terminals for loading and unloading intermodal containers from container ships.
Container cranes consist of a supporting framework that can traverse the length of a quay or yard on a rail track.

objects.title: Refractive index
objects.extract: In optics, the refractive index (also called refraction index or index of refraction), often denoted n, is the ratio of the speed of light in vacuum (c) to the speed of light in a given optical medium (v), n=c/v. The refractive index determines how much the path of light is bent, or refracted, when entering a material, as described by Snell's law of refracti

objects.title: Sewing machine
objects.extract: A sewing machine is a machine used to sew fabric and materials together with thread. Sewing machines were invented during the first Industrial Revolution to decrease the amount of manual sewing work performed in clothing companies.

objects.title: Fire damper
objects.extract: Fire dampers (or fire shutters) are passive fire protection products used in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) ducts to prevent and isolate the spread of fire inside the ductwork through fire-resistance rated walls and floors. Fire/smoke dampers are similar to fire dampers in fire resistance rating, and also prevent the spread of smoke inside

objects.title: Benkan
objects.description: Type of crown worn by Japanese emperors
objects.extract: The benkan (冕冠; lit. 'crown') is a type of crown traditionally worn by Japanese emperors and crown princes. It is also called tama no kōburi (玉乃冠; lit. 'jeweled crown').

objects.title: Lesser sac-winged bat
objects.description: Species of bat in the family Emballonuridae
objects.extract: The lesser sac-winged bat or lesser white-lined bat (Saccopteryx leptura) is a bat species of the family Emballonuridae from South and Middle America.

objects.title: Kingfisher
objects.extract: Kingfishers are a family, the Alcedinidae, of small to medium-sized, brightly coloured birds in the order Coraciiformes. They have a cosmopolitan distribution: most species live in the tropical regions of Africa, Asia, and Oceania, but they can also be found in Europe and the Americas.

objects.title: Wheelchair
objects.description: Chair with wheels used by people with mobility deficiencies
objects.extract: A wheelchair is a mobilized form of chair using two or more wheels, a footrest, and an armrest usually cushioned. It is used when walking is difficult or impossible due to illnesses, injury, disabilities, or age-related health conditions.

objects.title: Barcelona chair
objects.description: Chair by Mies van der Rohe, Lilly Reich
objects.extract: The Barcelona chair is a chair designed by Ludwig Mies van der Rohe and Lilly Reich for the German Pavilion at the International Exposition of 1929, hosted in Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain. Knoll Inc.

objects.title: Baleen whale
objects.extract: Baleen whales (), also known as whalebone whales, are marine mammals of the parvorder Mysticeti in the infraorder Cetacea (whales, dolphins and porpoises), which use baleen plates (or "whalebone") in their mouths to sieve plankton from the water. Mysticeti comprises the families Balaenidae (right and bowhead whales), Balaenopteridae (rorquals), Eschrichtiida

objects.title: Christmas lights
objects.extract: Christmas lights (also known as fairy lights, festive lights or string lights) are lights often used for decoration in celebration of Christmas, often on display throughout the Christmas season including Advent and Christmastide.  The custom goes back to when Christmas trees were decorated with candles, which symbolized Christ being the light of the world.

objects.title: Cod
objects.description: Common name for several fish, but mainly the demersal genus Gadus
objects.extract: Cod (pl.: cod) is the common name for the demersal fish genus Gadus, belonging to the family Gadidae. Cod is also used as part of the common name for several other fish species, and one species that belongs to genus Gadus is not commonly called cod (Alaska pollock, Gadus chalcogrammus).

objects.title: Ommatotriton vittatus
objects.extract: The southern banded newt (Ommatotriton vittatus) is a species of salamander in the family Salamandridae found in Armenia (Lori Province of northern Armenia), Iraq, Israel, Jordan, Lebanon, Syria, and Turkey. This species has two subspecies - O. v.

objects.title: Leaf blower
objects.description: Machine used to blow leaves and debris
objects.extract: A leaf blower, commonly known as a blower, is a device that propels air out of a nozzle to move debris such as leaves and grass cuttings.  Leaf blowers are powered by electric or gasoline motors.

objects.title: Twin Bing
objects.extract: The Twin Bing is a candy bar made by the Palmer Candy Company of Sioux City, Iowa.  It consists of two round, chewy, cherry-flavored nougats coated with a mixture of chopped peanuts and chocolate.

objects.title: Backgammon
objects.description: Board and dice game for two players
objects.extract: Backgammon is a two-player board game played with counters and dice on tables boards. It is the most widespread Western member of the large family of tables games, whose ancestors date back at least 1,600 years.

objects.title: Minsk Tractor Works
objects.extract: Minsk Tractor Works (Belarusian: Мінскі трактарны завод, romanized: Minski traktarny zavod; Russian: Минский тракторный завод, romanized: Minskiy traktorniy zavod; MTZ) is a Belarusian agricultural machinery manufacturer with headquarters in Minsk, Belarus. Minsk Tractor Works is one of the main tractor factories in the country.

objects.title: List of grape varieties
objects.artist: Please note: This photo can be reproduced. Please quote the source as indicated

objects.title: Thimble
objects.extract: A thimble is a small pitted cup worn on the finger that protects it from being pricked or poked by a needle while sewing. The Old English word þȳmel, the ancestor of thimble, is derived from Old English þūma, the ancestor of the English word thumb.

objects.title: List of fur headgear
objects.extract: Below is a list of fur headgear (fur hats, fur caps, etc.). Some other kinds of hats can be lined or trimmed with fur, such as historical Scandinavian  karpus or kabuss/kabuds hats, or Spanish montera/montero caps.

objects.title: Pano (De Boneca?)
objects.description: item of the collection of toys of the Museu Paulista

objects.title: Cinetorhynchus reticulatus
objects.extract: Cinetorhynchus reticulatus is a species of shrimp belonging to the family Rhynchocinetidae, known by the common name green-eye dancing shrimp or reticulated hinge-beak shrimp.

objects.title: Knox Automobile Company
objects.extract: The Knox Automobile Company was a manufacturer of automobiles in Springfield, Massachusetts, United States, between 1900 and 1914. Knox also built trucks and farm tractors until 1924.

objects.title: Vodka
objects.extract: Vodka (Polish: wódka [ˈvutka]; Russian: водка [ˈvotkə]) is a clear distilled alcoholic beverage. Its varieties originated in Poland and Russia.

objects.title: Damascening
objects.extract: Damascening is the art of inlaying different metals into one another—typically, gold or silver into a darkly oxidized steel background—to produce intricate patterns similar to niello. The English term comes from a perceived resemblance to the rich tapestry patterns of damask silk.

objects.title: Yellow rice
objects.description: Rice dish made yellow with spices
objects.extract: Yellow rice is a traditional yellow-colored rice dish in Iranian, Middle East, Moroccan, Ecuadorian, Peruvian, Caribbean, Portuguese, Filipino, Afghan, Indian, Sri Lankan, South African and Indonesian cuisines. It is made using white rice turned yellow by annatto, saffron or turmeric.

objects.title: Heterixalus luteostriatus
objects.extract: Heterixalus luteostriatus is a species of frog in the family Hyperoliidae endemic to Madagascar. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, swamps, freshwater marshes, intermittent freshwater marshes, arable land, urban areas, heavily degraded former forests, ponds, irrigated land, and seasonally flooded agricultural land.

objects.title: Stephanoxis lalandi
objects.extract: The green-crowned plovercrest, also black-breasted plovercrest  or simply plovercrest,  (Stephanoxis lalandi) is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is endemic to Brazil.
objects.artist: Dario Sanches from São Paulo, Brazil

objects.title: Henna
objects.extract: Henna is a dye made from dried, powdered leaves of Lawsonia inermis, used to produce reddish stains used in body art. It originates in North Africa and the Middle East, and has been used since at least antiquity across the region, including in ancient Egypt, as well as in ancient Mesopotamia and broader pre-Islamic Arabian cultures, as a hair and body dye, n

objects.title: Senegal Thick-knee
objects.extract: The Senegal thick-knee (Burhinus senegalensis) is a stone-curlew, a group of waders in the family Burhinidae. Their vernacular scientific name refers to the prominent joints in the long yellow or greenish legs.

objects.title: Lacquerware
objects.extract: Lacquerware are objects decoratively covered with lacquer. Lacquerware includes small or large containers, tableware, miscellaneous lacquered small objects carried by people, and larger objects such as furniture and even coffins painted with lacquer.

objects.title: Welding helmet
objects.description: Helmet that protects eyes during welding
objects.extract: A welding helmet is a piece of personal protective equipment used by welders to protect the user from concentrated light and flying particles. Different welding processes need stronger lens shades with auto-darkening filters, while goggles suffice for others.

objects.title: Broomball
objects.extract: Broomball is a both a recreational and organized competitive winter and ball sport played on ice or snow. It is played either indoors or outdoors, depending on the climate and location.

objects.title: FOS.54
objects.description: Phyllolepis concentricus fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Comb
objects.description: Toothed device used for styling, cleaning and managing hair and scalp
objects.extract: A comb is a tool consisting of a shaft that holds a row of teeth for pulling through the hair to clean, untangle, or style it.  Combs have been used since prehistoric times, having been discovered in very refined forms from settlements dating back to 5,000 years ago in Persia.

objects.title: Pelargonium
objects.extract: Pelargonium () is a genus of flowering plants commonly called geraniums, pelargoniums, or storksbills. It includes about 280 species of perennials, succulents, and shrubs.

objects.title: Honda Civic
objects.extract: The Honda Civic (Japanese: ホンダ・シビック, Hepburn: Honda Shibikku) is a series of automobiles manufactured by Honda since 1972. As of 2023, the Civic is positioned between the Honda Fit/City and Honda Accord in Honda's global passenger car line-up.

objects.title: Chrysanthemum
objects.description: Genus of flowering plants in the daisy family
objects.extract: Chrysanthemums ( kriss-AN-thə-məmz), sometimes abbreviated to mums or chrysanths, are perennial herbaceous flowering plants in the family Asteraceae that bloom in the autumn. They are native to East Asia and northeastern Europe.
objects.artist: A.Davey from Portland, Oregon, EE UU

objects.title: Blacktip reef shark
objects.extract: The blacktip reef shark (Carcharhinus melanopterus) is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, which can be easily identified by the prominent black tips on its fins (especially on the first dorsal fin and its caudal fin). Among the most abundant sharks inhabiting the tropical coral reefs of the Indian and Pacific Oceans, this species prefe

objects.title: Aloe
objects.extract: Aloe (; also written Aloë) is a genus containing over 650 species of flowering succulent plants. The most widely known  species is Aloe vera, or "true aloe".

objects.title: Bowl
objects.description: Round, open-top container frequently used as tableware
objects.extract: A bowl is a typically round dish or container generally used for preparing, serving, storing, or consuming food. The interior of a bowl is characteristically shaped like a spherical cap, with the edges and the bottom, forming a seamless curve.

objects.title: Engine crane
objects.extract: An engine crane (also referred as engine hoist) is a common repair tool used in vehicle repair shops to remove or install gasoline or diesel engines in small and crowded vehicle engine compartments. It uses a heavy cantilevered support structure to hold the engine in mid-air so that the mechanic can carefully connect or disconnect fragile hoses and wires on

objects.title: Leporidae
objects.extract: Leporidae () is the family of rabbits and hares (Lepus), containing over 70 species of extant mammals in all. Together with the pikas, the Leporidae constitute the mammalian order Lagomorpha.

objects.title: Jerrycan
objects.extract: A jerrycan or jerrican (also styled jerry can or jerri can) is a fuel container made from pressed steel (and more recently, high density polyethylene). It was designed in Germany in the 1930s for military use to hold 20 litres (4.4 imp gal; 5.3 US gal) of fuel or water, and saw widespread use by both Germany and the Allies during the Second World War.

objects.title: Currywurst
objects.description: Fast food dish of German origin
objects.extract: Currywurst (German: [ˈkœʁiˌvʊɐ̯st] ) is a fast food dish of German origin consisting of sausage (usually pork) with curry ketchup. It was invented in 1949 by Herta Heuwer, who began selling it at a food stand in West Berlin.

objects.title: Cowrie
objects.description: Common name for a group of sea snails
objects.extract:
Cowrie or cowry (pl. cowries) is the common name for a group of small to large sea snails in the family Cypraeidae.
Cowrie shells have held cultural, economic, and ornamental significance in various cultures.

objects.title: heatball
objects.description: brand name for an incandescent lamp
objects.extract: Heatball is a brand name for an incandescent lamp. The brand was used as part of a scheme by Siegfried Rotthäuser, a mechanical engineer from Essen in Germany, to stimulate discussion of EU Regulation 244/2009.

objects.title: Raised pavement marker
objects.extract: A raised pavement marker is a safety device used on roads. These devices are usually made with plastic, ceramic, thermoplastic paint, glass or occasionally metal, and come in a variety of shapes and colors.

objects.title: Jeans
objects.description: Pants/trousers made from denim or dungaree cloth
objects.extract: Jeans are a type of trousers made from denim or dungaree cloth. Often the term "jeans" refers to a particular style of trousers, called "blue jeans", with the addition of copper pocket rivets added by Jacob W. Davis in 1871 and patented by Davis and Levi Strauss on May 20, 1873.

objects.title: Sailor cap
objects.description: Hat worn by enlisted Naval personnel
objects.extract: A sailor cap is a round, flat visorless hat worn by sailors in many of the world's navies. A tally, an inscribed black silk ribbon, is tied around the base which usually bears the name of a ship or a navy.

objects.title: Chamaea fasciata
objects.extract: The wrentit (Chamaea fasciata) is a small bird that lives in chaparral, oak woodlands, and bushland on the western coast of North America. It is the only species in the genus Chamaea.
objects.artist: Becky Matsubara from El Sobrante, California

objects.title: Crown of thorns
objects.extract: According to the New Testament, a woven crown of thorns (Ancient Greek: στέφανος ἐξ ἀκανθῶν, romanized: stephanos ex akanthōn or ἀκάνθινος στέφανος, akanthinos stephanos) was placed on the head of Jesus during the events leading up to his crucifixion. It was one of the instruments of the Passion, employed by Jesus' captors both to cause him pain and to mock

objects.title: Knitting needle
objects.extract: A knitting needle or knitting pin is a tool in hand-knitting to produce knitted fabrics.  They generally have a long shaft and taper at their end, but they are not nearly as sharp as sewing needles.

objects.title: Pocket trumpet
objects.extract: The pocket trumpet is a B♭ or C trumpet that is constructed with the tubing wound into a much smaller coil than a standard trumpet, generally with a smaller-diameter bell. It is not a standard instrument in a concert band or orchestra and is generally regarded as a novelty.

objects.title: Votive offering
objects.description: Object placed or left somewhere for religious purposes
objects.extract: A votive offering or votive deposit is one or more objects displayed or deposited, without the intention of recovery or use, in a sacred place for religious purposes. Such items are a feature of modern and ancient societies and are generally made to gain favor with supernatural forces.

objects.title: Hyperolius nasutus
objects.extract: Hyperolius nasutus is a species of frog in the family Hyperoliidae. Common names include long-nosed reed frog, sharp-nosed reed frog and long reed frog.

objects.title: Lamina minor
objects.extract: Lamina minor is a species of Toxopidae spider that is endemic to New Zealand.

objects.title: Laundry
objects.description: Washing of clothing and other textiles
objects.extract: Laundry is the washing of clothing and other textiles, and, more broadly, their drying and ironing as well. Laundry has been part of history since humans began to wear clothes, so the methods by which different cultures have dealt with this universal human need are of interest to several branches of scholarship.

objects.title: FOS.76
objects.description: Coccosteus cuspidatus fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Krispy Kreme
objects.description: US-based doughnut company and coffeehouse chain
objects.extract: Krispy Kreme, Inc. (previously Krispy Kreme Doughnuts, Inc.) is an American multinational doughnut company and coffeehouse chain.

objects.title: Boeing 747
objects.description: American wide-body four-engined jet long-range aircraft
objects.extract:
The Boeing 747 is a long-range wide-body airliner designed and manufactured by Boeing Commercial Airplanes in the United States between 1968 and 2023.
It was conceived in response to the demand of Pan Am for a jet 2+1⁄2 times the size of the 707, which had been introduced in October 1958, to reduce the airline's seat cost by 30%.

objects.title: Breakfast cereal
objects.extract:
Breakfast cereal is a category of food, including food products, made from processed cereal grains, that are eaten as part of breakfast or as a snack food, primarily in Western societies.
Although warm, cooked cereals like oat meal, maize grits, and wheat farina have the longest history as traditional breakfast foods, branded and ready-to-eat cold cereals (m

objects.title: Tiento
objects.description: ball supplied by Argentina for the first-half of 1930 FIFA World Cup final

objects.title: Textile arts
objects.description: Form of arts and crafts using fibers
objects.extract: Textile art is art created from natural or synthetic fibers or from fabric or textile. Textile art is synonymous with fiber art.
objects.artist: Unknown creatorUnknown creator, Nasca Culture, Peru

objects.title: Micro Bit
objects.description: Single-board computer designed by the BBC
objects.extract: The Micro Bit (also referred to as BBC Micro Bit or stylized as micro:bit) is an open source hardware ARM-based embedded system designed by the BBC for use in computer education in the United Kingdom. It was first announced on the launch of BBC's Make It Digital campaign on 12 March 2015 with the intent of delivering 1 million devices to pupils in the UK. Th

objects.title: Graïle
objects.extract: The graïle, (or Occitan graile) is a woodwind instrument of Languedoc, France, resembling a oboe. It is played in Monts de Lacaune (in the department of Tarn) and surrounding areas including Bezime.

objects.title: Anthony van Dyck
objects.extract:
Sir Anthony van Dyck (; Dutch: Antoon van Dijck [ˈɑntoːɱ‿vɑn ˈdɛik]; 22 March 1599 – 9 December 1641) was a Flemish Baroque artist, who became the leading court painter in England after success in the Spanish Netherlands and Italy.
The seventh child of Frans van Dyck, a wealthy silk merchant in Antwerp, Anthony painted from an early age.
objects.artist: After Anthony van Dyck / Anthony van Dyck

objects.title: Brogue shoe
